summary: GoalPilot AI — iteration 2 (3 new features). Backend: 15/15 pytest tests pass (100%) for /api/nudge, /api/calendar/url, /api/calendar/export.ics, and /api/subscription/upgrade with annual billing + invalid-billing 400. Frontend: nudge banner (testID nudge-card + nudge-quick-win) renders on Dashboard from /api/nudge; Pricing toggle (pricing-toggle-monthly / pricing-toggle-annual) flips prices correctly ($12/$29 monthly ↔ $9/$21 annual with Save $36 / Save $96 / Billed $108-yr / $252-yr); Profile shows Sync-to-Calendar row (profile-calendar-btn) which fetches a JWT token and opens /api/calendar/export.ics?token=… in a new tab; Profile upgrade card shows 'Save 25% with annual plans · Pro from $9/mo · Coach from $21/mo'. Regression flows (login, dashboard stats, tasks/today) all green.

issues: Console warns: 'shadow*' style props deprecated, use 'boxShadow' (non-blocking, carried over from iter 1)

react_native_issues.issue: Login form is missing testIDs login-email / login-password / login-submit (Playwright had to fall back to nth-input + text match). Not blocking — login works — but inconsistent with the testID coverage on Dashboard / Pricing / Profile. Recommend adding for future regression.

action_items: (Optional) Add testIDs login-email, login-password, login-submit on app/login.tsx to align with the testID conventions used elsewhere.
(Carried over) Replace shadow* style props with boxShadow and Image style.resizeMode with prop resizeMode to silence deprecation warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/nudge: days_since computed from datetime.fromisoformat(last['completed_at']).date() — completed_at is stored as full ISO timestamp with timezone (datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()), parsing OK. Edge case: if a task is completed in user's local 'today' but the stored UTC date is 'tomorrow' for a user east of UTC, days_since may show 0 or even -1; consider storing user-tz or using completed_at >= today-UTC threshold.

server.py /api/calendar/export.ics: auth via JWT in query string is fine for calendar subscriptions, but the same access token used by the app is reused — anyone with the URL has full app access if leaked. Consider issuing a scoped, long-lived 'calendar:read' token instead of reusing the access JWT.

server.py /api/calendar/export.ics: ICS lines are joined with \r\n (correct per RFC 5545) but no line-folding for long SUMMARY/DESCRIPTION values — Outlook may truncate values >75 octets. Low priority for MVP.

pricing.tsx: useState<Cycle>('annual') default is good for highlighting savings. Save labels ($36 / $96) are static strings — if monthly prices ever change, two values must be kept in sync. Worth deriving from (monthly*12 - annual*12).
Profile sync flow: window.open is called BEFORE the Alert.alert('Calendar sync', …). On native this is fine, but on web the alert can mask the new tab. Minor UX nit.
seed_data_creation: Reused seeded user tester@goalpilot.ai / Test@1234. Test temporarily upgraded plan to pro/annual then coach/monthly, then reset back to free/monthly at end of run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two backend test files are now in /app/backend/tests/: test_goalpilot_api.py (auth, goals+AI, tasks, dashboard, weekly review, mock upgrade) and test_new_features.py (nudge, calendar JWT + ICS, annual billing, invalid billing 400, regression). Both rely on EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL env var. Frontend tabs path is /(tabs)/dashboard|goals|review|profile rendered as plain /dashboard|/goals|/review|/profile in expo-router. After hard navigation to /pricing, give Metro ~4-6s to finish bundling — first navigation can briefly show stale content during HMR. Stripe upgrade endpoint is MOCKED (no real Stripe call); /api/subscription/upgrade just persists user.plan + user.billing.
rca_of_issue: No blocking bugs. Earlier transient failure where the pricing page rendered without the toggle was a stale Metro bundle right after `supervisorctl restart expo`; a hard reload (?bust=N) showed the new toggle and prices correctly, confirming the source code is good — main agent can ignore this transient.
